insight:
Hey! Yesterday was a solid recovery day with 7,161 steps and no formal training - perfect timing just 8 days out from Chester. Your body burned 2,383 calories naturally, showing your metabolism is firing well. However, that 5.6 hours of sleep is concerning this close to race day, and we need to address this immediately.

Your HRV at 101ms sits beautifully in your optimal 92-114ms range, and that 46 bpm resting heart rate shows excellent cardiovascular fitness. With stress levels low at 30/100, your recovery systems are functioning well despite the short sleep. Today's training can proceed as planned - your body is ready for whatever we've scheduled.

Focus heavily on sleep hygiene tonight. Given your low step count yesterday, get some gentle movement today to promote better sleep quality, but avoid anything intense after 6pm. Prioritize 7-8 hours tonight - quality sleep is your secret weapon for race day performance.
